title: IRS Code 846 Meaning (Refund Issued) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 846 means a refund was issued. Learn what 846 signals, how to verify refund amounts and timing, and which codes can still change the outcome.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 846 Meaning

IRS Code 846 is the IRS posting for “refund issued”. Each 846 line means the IRS released an overpayment as a refund. This page shows how to read 846 in context, verify timing and amounts, and spot the codes that can still reroute or reverse the refund.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 846?

Plain-English definition: Code 846 means the IRS issued a refund. It posts when the account shows an overpayment and the IRS releases funds.

Common transcript type
Account Transcript
Usually appears near
Codes 150, 766/768, 810
Typical context
Overpayments, refund release, delivery timing
Client-friendly summary
“The IRS sent your refund”

When does Code 846 appear?

  1. After credits and payments post: Credits (like 766/768) and payments reduce the balance; if an overpayment exists and no blocks remain, 846 posts.
  2. After holds are cleared: Refund freezes or reviews can delay issuance; 846 typically posts after the account is cleared to release funds.
  3. After adjustments: Amended return activity or recalculations can create a later overpayment and trigger 846 later in the cycle.

What does Code 846 usually signal?

  • Refund released: The IRS issued the refund amount shown on the 846 line.
  • Timing is not instant: The 846 date is the issuance date, not the moment money hits the bank.
  • Outcome can still change: Later codes can show returned refunds, cancellations, offsets, or transfers.

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Confirm the amount and date: Match the 846 amount to expected refund math and transcript activity.
  2. Scan for downstream changes: Look for 740/841 (returned/cancelled) and 820/826 (transfers/offsets) after 846.
  3. Align client expectations: Set clear delivery expectations for direct deposit vs mailed check and typical delays.

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how Code 846 appears in a client-ready transcript report:

Refund issuance example
Code 150 – Return filed and assessed
Date: 04/08/2024
Code 768 – Earned income credit
Amount: -$2,700
Code 766 – Credit applied to account
Amount: -$1,000
Code 846 – Refund issued
Amount: $500

FAQ

What does IRS Code 846 mean on an IRS transcript?

IRS Code 846 indicates the IRS issued a refund. It posts when the account shows an overpayment and the IRS releases funds.

Is the Code 846 date the deposit date or the IRS send date?

The 846 date is the IRS issuance date. The deposit or check delivery can occur a few days later.
